topic.what: Self-synchronizing tokenization disambiguation framework for preventing sender-receiver desynchronization in generative linguistic steganography
topic.how: Monitors receiver-view tokenization during generation and triggers corrective reset only when ambiguity is detected, confining errors to sparse residual bit flips rather than global extraction failure
topic.why_matters: Resolves tension between security (distributional preservation) and efficiency in GLS tokenization disambiguation; existing methods either distort generation distribution or incur prohibitive runtime overhead

characteristics.what_it_measures: Distributional security: deviation of token distribution from baseline steganographic scheme
characteristics.evidence: Table 1/2: ReTokSync achieves 0.00 average and maximum KLD across all top-k settings, matching baseline exactly
characteristics.context: Zero KL divergence confirmed on both Llama-3.1-8B (English) and Qwen3-8B (Chinese)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Bit-wise accuracy of message recovery at receiver
characteristics.vs_baseline: Discop without disambiguation fails completely (marked as -)
characteristics.context: Residual errors are sparse bit flips; 100% recovery achievable with two-channel correction mechanism

characteristics.what_itasures: Computational overhead relative to baseline Discop
characteristics.context: Dramatically lower overhead than all competing disambiguation methods while maintaining zero KL divergence

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average bits embedded per generated token
characteristics.evidence: Table 1: 2.28 (k=32), 2.95 (k=128), 3.50 (k=512) on English; identical to baseline Discop capacity
characteristics.context: Capacity preserved at baseline level vs. Syncpool's 52-85% capacity reduction

characteristics.what_it_measures: Efficiency of converting available embedding entropy into payload bits
characteristics.evidence: Table 1/2: ReTokSync utilization matches baseline Discop (0.88-0.91) vs. Syncpool (0.14-0.69)
characteristics.context: Near-optimal entropy utilization without sacrificing distributional security

characteristics.what_it_measures: Residual bit error rate after primary channel transmission in two-channel setup
characteristics.context: Errors remain highly sparse; fully correctable with auxiliary channel at <1% correction-to-embedding ratio

good_for: high-security covert channels requiring distributional preservation
not_for: Applications requiring zero residual error without auxiliary channel
requires: Shared tokenizer between sender and receiver
BPE or subword tokenization scheme with prefix tokens
Ability to perform online re-tokenization during generation

novelty: Self-synchronizing disambiguation: monitors receiver-view tokenization and triggers correction only when ambiguity occurs
Zero KL divergence with <2% time overhead: resolves security-efficiency tension
Two-channel covert communication: ReTokSync primary + Syncpool auxiliary for 100% recovery
Ambiguity sparsity insight: <1% of tokens trigger ambiguity despite high sample-level frequency
open_problems: Generalization beyond BPE tokenizers to other schemes
Cross-lingual transfer and robustness to tokenizer differences
